channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ASEAN still divided on whether to recognize Myanmar’s recent elections
item.description: Wala pang pagkakasundo ang Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) kung kikilalanin ba ang resulta ng kamakailang halalan sa Myanmar, ayon sa pahayag ng ASEAN 2026 spokesperson na si Dominic Xa...

item.title: Billie Eilish speaks out on aging, body image, and rejecting cosmetic surgery
item.description: Billie Eilish has shared her perspective on aging and cosmetic surgery, emphasizing her desire to grow older naturally without altering her appearance. Speaking on Amy Poehler’s podcast Good Hang, the...

item.title: Former IGP Usman Alkali joins Yobe 2027 governorship race after consensus talks fail
item.description: Former Inspector‑General of Police Usman Alkali has officially entered the race for the 2027 Yobe State governorship election, rejecting the earlier consensus plan that party leaders wanted to use to ...

item.title: Joanna Lumley and Lucy Punch Discuss the Return of the Comedy Series Amandaland
item.description: The hit British comedy Amandaland returns, shifting the focus from Julia to the pretentious Amanda, played by Lucy Punch, and her mother Felicity, portrayed by Joanna Lumley. Following the success of ...

item.title: UK government borrowing costs hit highest levels in decades amid global uncertainty
item.description: Long-term borrowing costs for the UK government have reached their highest levels since 1998, as the conflict in Iran and domestic political uncertainty ahead of upcoming local and national elections ...

item.title: Trump-backed candidates win key Indiana GOP primaries, showing influence over party
item.description: In the Indiana primary elections on May 5, 2026, President Donald Trump demonstrated his continuing influence over the Republican Party by successfully supporting challengers who unseated a majority o...

item.title: Trump temporarily halts US naval escorts in Strait of Hormuz amid peace talks with Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced a brief pause in the operation to escort commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz, citing progress towards a possible agreement with Iran. The strait, a critic...

item.title: AGL Forecasts Higher Profit Amid Renewable Energy Transition and AI Demand
item.description: AGL Energy has revised its profit forecast upwards, now expecting an underlying net profit after tax of between $610 million and $680 million, up from the previous estimate of $580 million to $680 mil...

item.title: Pakistan steps up coordination with provinces on climate adaptation and disaster risk reduction
item.description: The second edition of the Breathe Pakistan International Climate Change Conference is underway in Islamabad, bringing together policymakers, experts, and stakeholders to address the country’s growing ...

item.title: Profile of Agnimitra Paul: BJP’s Potential Candidate for West Bengal Chief Minister
item.description: With West Bengal assembly elections concluding in 2026, speculation is mounting over who will assume the Chief Minister’s post as Mamata Banerjee has refused to resign. Among the frontrunners from the...

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship Leads to Multiple Deaths
item.description: Three passengers aboard a luxury cruise ship have tragically died due to a hantavirus outbreak, with four additional cases confirmed or suspected. Hantaviruses are viruses carried by rodents and can i...

item.title: Amol Rajan to Take Leave from Today Programme to Participate in Celebrity Traitors
item.description: Amol Rajan, a longstanding presenter on BBC Radio 4's Today programme since 2021, has been granted time off to participate in the upcoming series of The Celebrity Traitors. The decision has sparked co...

item.title: CTV Calgary Earns Ten Nominations in RTDNA Awards
item.description: CTV Calgary has been recognized with ten nominations for the 2026 RTDNA Awards, highlighting its commitment to excellence in journalism. The nominations span a range of categories, reflecting the stat...

item.title: London police increase security for Jewish communities following recent arrests
item.description: Authorities in London have announced the deployment of additional police officers to protect Jewish communities amid rising concerns over safety. This move follows a series of arrests related to threa...

item.title: Supreme Court Clarifies Jurisdiction Division Between SC and Federal Constitutional Court
item.description: The Supreme Court of Pakistan has formally clarified the roles and jurisdictions of the Supreme Court (SC) and the newly-formed Federal Constitutional Court (FCC) following the 27th Constitutional Ame...

item.title: Tanzania and Kenya urged to create single economic system for growth
item.description: Tanzanian businessman Rostam Azizi has pushed for a deeper economic partnership between Tanzania and Kenya, saying it is key to unlocking investment, boosting growth, and securing opportunities for th...

item.title: Iran Offers to Reopen Strait of Hormuz Ahead of Nuclear Negotiations, Proposal Rejected by U.S.
item.description: An Iranian proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the U.S. blockade has been rejected by President Donald Trump, according to a senior Iranian official. The plan suggested that shipping in th...

item.title: De Lima denies scripting statements of Madriaga in VP Sara Duterte case
item.description: The claim that House Senior Deputy Minority Leader Leila de Lima provided a script to Ramil Madriaga, alleged former bagman of Vice President Sara Duterte, during his jail visit is FALSE. De Lima did ...

item.title: RBA Interest Rate Decision Explained Amid Inflation and Global Tensions
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) recently announced its latest interest rate decision, highlighting the ongoing pressures on Australian household budgets. The announcement cited stubborn inflation ...

item.title: Cadet Kaye Demisana Librada from Batangas Tops PMA Class of 2026
item.description: Cadet 1st Class Christine Kaye Demisana Librada of Lipa City, Batangas, has been named the valedictorian of the Philippine Military Academy (PMA) Class of 2026. She is the eighth female cadet to achie...
